items.name: Engineering Tips
items.title: Engineering Tips
items.description: Curated engineering guidance grouped by domain.

Tips.Tip: Prefer clear, descriptive names over brevity
Tips.Why: Readable code reduces onboarding time and lowers the risk of misinterpretation during maintenance.

Tips.Tip: Write small, composable functions with single responsibilities
Tips.Why: Smaller units are easier to test, reason about, and change without unintended side effects.

Tips.Tip: Document decisions, not mechanics
Tips.Why: Rationale and trade-offs help future maintainers understand context and avoid repeating past mistakes.

Tips.Tip: Treat tests as part of the API
Tips.Why: Well-structured tests communicate expected behavior and protect critical flows from regressions.

Tips.Tip: Fail fast with explicit validation and error handling
Tips.Why: Clear errors shorten debugging cycles and improve reliability in production.

Tips.Tip: Prefer deterministic builds and reproducible environments
Tips.Why: Reproducibility makes CI trustworthy and reduces "it works on my machine" failures.

Tips.Tip: Keep dependencies current with a review cadence
Tips.Why: Planned upgrades reduce security exposure and avoid disruptive catch-up work.

Tips.Tip: Use version control for all work, including experiments
Tips.Why: History enables safe iteration, easier collaboration, and rapid rollback.

Tips.Tip: Review code for correctness, readability, and operability
Tips.Why: Balanced reviews catch defects and ensure code can be supported in production.

Tips.Tip: Design APIs with stability and clarity in mind
Tips.Why: Predictable interfaces reduce downstream churn and simplify integrations.

Tips.Tip: Instrument critical paths with metrics and logs
Tips.Why: Operational visibility is essential for troubleshooting and capacity planning.

Tips.Tip: Automate repetitive tasks with scripts or tooling
Tips.Why: Automation reduces human error and frees time for higher-impact work.

Tips.Tip: Establish clear ownership for services and components
Tips.Why: Ownership improves response times and encourages long-term stewardship.

Tips.Tip: Use meaningful branch names and structured commits
Tips.Why: Good metadata makes code history searchable and change intent clear.

Tips.Tip: Adopt linting and formatting as part of CI
Tips.Why: Consistent style reduces review noise and prevents avoidable defects.

Tips.Tip: Prefer migrations and feature flags for risky changes
Tips.Why: Controlled rollouts reduce downtime and allow fast rollback.

Tips.Tip: Back up critical data and test restores
Tips.Why: Backups only matter if recovery is proven and timely.

Tips.Tip: Optimize after measuring, not before
Tips.Why: Data-driven performance work prevents wasted effort and unexpected regressions.

Tips.Tip: Use containerization or standard runbooks for services
Tips.Why: Consistent execution environments simplify deployment and incident response.

Tips.Tip: Keep documentation close to the code
Tips.Why: Proximity increases the chance that docs stay current and actionable.

Tips.Tip: Prefer prompt-as-workflow for multi-step AI tasks
Tips.Why: Structured steps with explicit inputs and outputs keep automation deterministic and auditable.

Tips.Tip: Declare a single output type per artifact
Tips.Why: Separating machine prompts from narrative avoids ambiguity and aligns with output contracts.

Tips.Tip: Bound the work with explicit step and file limits
Tips.Why: Guardrails prevent scope creep and enforce manageable iteration sizes.

Tips.Tip: Make AI outputs repo-scoped and tool-oriented
Tips.Why: Concrete paths and CLI steps are easier to execute and review than vague guidance.

Tips.Tip: Capture rationale when generating workflows
Tips.Why: Decision context helps reviewers understand why a step exists and what it protects.

Tips.Tip: Avoid hidden side effects in AI-generated changes
Tips.Why: Explicitly declared modifications make reviews faster and reduce risk.

Tips.Tip: Use deterministic language and avoid recursive prompt chains
Tips.Why: Determinism improves repeatability and reduces unexpected tool behavior.

Tips.Tip: Separate execution from artifact generation
Tips.Why: Keeping agents in artifact-only mode preserves safety boundaries.

items.name: Identity
items.title: Identity Profile
items.description: Mission, philosophy, and operating model snapshot.

metadata.motto: Unit Ready. My space. My frequency. Orbit Achieved.

core_mission.statement: Mastering complexity with calm strategy; making the hard stuff easy.
core_mission.philosophy: Automation isn't about moving objects; it's about warping the environment until the result is a constant.

the_standard.desc: Universal, secure, stateless deployment environment; multi-cloud auth (GCP, AWS, Azure, Bitwarden).

the_blueprint.desc: Delivery engine automating versioning and multi-registry publishing.

the_catalyst.desc: Interface for instant Ignition; collapsing deployment complexity.

software_logistics.how: Rigorous Spectral Analysis before any unit enters orbit; payload verification.

performance_flow.geodesic_path: Shortest possible route from first line of code to production-ready state.
performance_flow.full_throttle: Instantaneous execution; immutable state once coordinates are set.

tenets.logic: Deployment never occurs if it risks breaking previous executions or active services.
Continuous deployment with total confidence via standardized worker units.
Decoupled logic for granular management of complex automation.
Declarative formats to minimize divergence between environments.
